title: Convene the ISEDC council and ratify the governed-judgment direction as a durable Decision Record
description: 7-seat ISEDC council (CTO/GC/CMO/CFO/CSO/CISO/VP-DevRel) reviews 107-RA-ANLY + 108-AT-ARCH. Rules on: LEAD Q layer-positioning (harness vs plane-above-harnesses); public-timing supersession of 030/001 dark-until-green (Jeremy DECIDED public — ratify guardrails, do not re-litigate); scope/sequence (Layer 1 first vs 1+2); category play (product + paper + Evidence-Bench scorecard); claim discipline vs banned terms; Q5 lock (paper OK, rival spec not); solo bandwidth; Glean/Notion first-mover risk; naming (Intendants -> Bob the Intendant). Output: 109-AT-DECR with verbatim seat positions + dissent preserved.
close_reason: Convened 7-seat ISEDC council (CTO/GC/CMO/CFO/CSO/CISO/VP-DevRel via iep-isedc-* agents). Filed 109-AT-DECR-isedc-governed-judgment-layer-2026-07-12.md with verbatim seat positions; session at ~/.claude/skills/exec-decision-council/sessions/2026-07-12-governed-judgment-layer/.

description: Convene 7-seat adversarial council per /exec-decision-council skill. All prior bindings (Session 1 Q1-Q5, Session 3 Q2-Q3) are reopenable. acceptance_criteria: (1) Decision Record DR-010-AT-DECR-isedc-council-session-4-widened-scope-2026-05-13.md landed in intent-eval-lab/000-docs/. (2) Verbatim seat positions preserved per ISEDC discipline. (3) Vote tally + primary tensions + dissent surface documented. (4) Q1-Q6 each have a locked decision + binding constraints. notes: User directive 2026-05-13 (acting head of board): 'Everything that is in the works right now needs to be intervened with the large plan we made. The large plan needs to incorporate filling the gaps of the work that we already started. The work that we started in this plan needs to fold into the new plan. If it needs to be rearranged and restructured, that is fine. We do not just stop what we are working on. We figure out a way to incorporate it into the new plan and adapt and overcome after you convene with the board. But we want the whole foundation for the whole thing figured out.' Mandate scope: FULL architectural lock. Prior-bindings scope: EVERYTHING REOPENABLE.
close_reason: ISEDC Session 4 convened 2026-05-13. Decision Record landed at intent-eval-lab/000-docs/010-AT-DECR-isedc-council-session-4-widened-scope-2026-05-13.md. title: Build Layer 1 — governed grounded-judgment eval (the ratified wedge) in bob-the-intendant
description: GREEN-LIT BUILD BEAD (the design-phase deliverable). title: M1 manual: DNSSEC + CAA on evals.intentsolutions.io + Porkbun + cosign roundtrip — Jeremy action
description: Jeremy manual action required. Per ISEDC v1 Q1 CISO binding: enable DNSSEC at Porkbun for intentsolutions.io. Pin CAA records to single CA before first signed in-toto Statement against evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1. After enabled, run cosign verify-attestation round-trip against synthetic Statement to prove the chain works. Off-ramp: if registrar fails, pause M1 — do not ship signed rows against half-hardened namespace.
notes: DNSSEC fully validated 2026-05-12 ~04:25 UTC. DS records live at .io registry (2371 13 2 98F71CBB...). AD bit set on Cloudflare resolver responses (validation chain works end-to-end). CISO binding from ISEDC v1 Q1 SATISFIED: zone signed (DNSKEY KSK+ZSK ECDSAP256), CAA pinned to letsencrypt.org with iodef, evals.intentsolutions.io A→167.86.106.29 propagated, DS at .io registry validates. Next: install cosign + run live verify-attestation round-trip against synthetic Statement to formally close M2→M3 transition gate.
close_reason: M2->M3 transition gate EMPIRICALLY CLEARED 2026-05-12 04:25 UTC. Full chain validated end-to-end against the live, DNSSEC-protected predicate URI namespace: (1) DNSSEC enabled at Porkbun UI by Jeremy; DS records live at .io registry (2371 13 2 ECDSAP256SHA256); AD bit set on Cloudflare resolver responses; (2) CAA pinned (issue+issuewild letsencrypt.org + iodef); (3) evals.intentsolutions.io A record propagated to 1.1.1.1+8.8.8.8; (4) j-rig emit-evidence (PR #46) produces valid in-toto Statement v1 with predicateType https://evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1; (5) cosign v2.4.1 attest-blob signs against the live URI; (6) cosign verify-blob-attestation returns 'Verified OK'. Local Rekor push deferred (would be permanent + production-track decision); the unsigned envelope path works for non-Rekor consumers. Cosign integration finding: cosign wraps predicate in its own Statement v0.1 outer envelope rather than v1 — this is cosign default behavior and is interoperable with GUAC/Rekor/SLSA tooling. Future work: emit-evidence --predicate-body-only mode for direct cosign integration. No Rekor entry pushed in this validation run; the chain WORKS, it's just not yet pinned to public transparency log.

title: Score the IS validator fairly for rich external skills using non-IS section names and a capabilities security block (ccp-side fix)
description: ccp-side validator change (claude-code-plugins). Two well-built external skills (Xquik-dev hermes-tweet 126 stars, x-twitter-scraper 82 stars) scored 66/73 in the IS validator ONLY because their section names (Instructions/Output/Error Handling) do not match our marketplace taxonomy and their capabilities security block is unknown to the validator — a taxonomy mismatch on our side, not a skill deficiency. Fix: credit equivalent non-IS section names and recognize the capabilities field, or publish the section-name convention. Mirror to a ccp bead + GitHub issue at the outreach checkpoint (ccp dolt store was unresponsive to writes 2026-06-30). Parent: iel-fta. Surfaced during the Xquik dogfood initiative.

title: Decide whether reasoning-model evaluation needs a DR-028 pre-registration addendum.
description: Methodology call: is bumping max_tokens / disabling thinking / adding format coaching for reasoning models a change that must be pre-registered (and possibly ISEDC-reviewed) before any official deepseek-v4-flash arm result is published? Capture as an AT-DECR if so. Default: keep raised-budget runs labeled exploratory until decided.

title: Rerun the deepseek-v4-flash Arm A with raised max_tokens and interpret vs the -98 truncation baseline.
description: Exploratory (NOT pre-registered): rerun a small slice (a few specimens across strata A/B/C, K=0) with --max-tokens ~4096, results to /tmp. Compare against the -98 artifact. Note whether the yaml-fence-wrapping behavior persists (a real model-format finding) and whether the score becomes competitive.

title: Add a configurable --max-tokens flag to the Arm A runner (default 1024 — preserves DR-028 pre-registration).
description: run-arm-a.py hardcodes client.complete(prompt, max_tokens=1024). Add a --max-tokens CLI flag defaulting to 1024 (so the official pre-registered experiment is byte-identical) that overrides the per-call budget. Enables reasoning models to emit a full answer without truncation. Add to arm-b for parity.

title: Fairly evaluate deepseek-v4-flash (a reasoning model) in the Phase A baseline arm.
description: The first deepseek-v4-flash Arm A run scored delta -98, but inspection showed that was an artifact: the model is reasoning-capable, so its hidden reasoning consumed the hardcoded max_tokens=1024 budget (stop_reason=length, answer truncated to 248 chars) and it wrapped frontmatter in a yaml code fence the scorer rejects. Goal: get a fair signal for deepseek-v4-flash by making max_tokens configurable (without changing the pre-registered DR-028 default), rerunning at a higher budget, and deciding whether reasoning-model eval warrants a pre-registration addendum.

title: Add deepseek-v4-flash as a Phase A.0 baseline provider (DeepSeek V4, OpenAI-compatible).
description: Wire DeepSeek V4 Flash into research/phase-a-0-baseline/scripts/_arm_common.py as a paid OpenAI-compatible provider (base https://api.deepseek.com/v1, model id deepseek-v4-flash, key DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, pricing $0.14 in / $0.28 out per MTok, 1M context). Mirrors GroqProvider/NVIDIAProvider. Adds to ALL_PROVIDER_NAMES + get_provider factory + unit/smoke tests; not free-tier. Key already present in .env.sops.
close_reason: deepseek-v4-flash provider shipped (PR #185 merged 2026-06-21): implemented, ruff+pytest green, verified live, Gemini review fix applied.

title: Research: trace-driven skill evolution as Evidence Bundle consumer — design + references
description:
Open question: how does the Intent Eval Platform consume agent-curated skill evolution? Self-improving skill ecosystems (the agentskills.io open-standard pattern) generate streams of skill diffs (new skills + patches to existing ones) at runtime; without a guardrail layer those diffs cannot be trusted.

This issue scopes the **research + design surface** before any code lands. The eval platform is uniquely positioned to be the trust layer: audit-harness gates the diff statically, j-rig-binary-eval scores the variant against baseline behaviorally, intent-rollout-gate signs the ship/no-ship decision.

## What we're studying

1. **Trace format** — can an Evidence Bundle (v0.1.0-draft) carry a skill-invocation trace? Inputs (user prompt, tool calls, final state) + outputs (skill diff, success signal). May need a new bundle subject type `skill-variant`.
2. **Variant generator** — out of scope for the platform. We consume diffs from any producer (a runtime agent, a DSPy/GEPA-style optimizer, a human author). Producer-agnostic by design.
3. **Guardrail stack composition:**
   - **audit-harness:** new gate `skill-diff-safety` (size limit, no escape patterns, no cache-busting frontmatter changes, hash-pin compatibility, semantic-preservation heuristic)
   - **j-rig-binary-eval:** existing 7-layer rubric runs against (baseline skill vs. variant skill) producing 14 yes/no signals
   - **intent-rollout-gate:** GHA action reads bundle, applies repo policy (e.g., "variant must pass all 7 layers AND not regress any baseline layer AND have human approval")
4. **Signature surface** — what does the in-toto Statement attest? Subject = skill-variant content hash; predicate = bundle URI.

## References for examples

- **agentskills.io open standard** — vendor-neutral skill format spec; the format every agent runtime is converging on
- **DSPy + GEPA** (ICLR 2026) — example of a trace-driven optimizer that proposes variants from execution traces (this is the kind of *producer* the eval platform would consume from); 5-guardrail pattern (test suite + size + cache compat + semantic preservation + human review) is the relevant prior art
- **Our existing j-rig-binary-eval/PRD.md** — the 7-layer binary rubric is already designed for skill grading
- **Our existing audit-harness scripts/** — escape-scan, harness-hash, bias-count are reusable
- **Evidence Bundle v0.1.0-draft schema** (this repo: specs/evidence-bundle/) — would need extension for skill-variant subjects
- **Cross-ref CCP beads:** claude-nwdy (progressive disclosure), claude-22cg (conditional visibility), and the env-var-self-decl bead — those define the skill format the platform evaluates

## Acceptance (research phase)

- specs/skill-evolution/v0.1.0-draft/RESEARCH.md authored — scope, prior art, non-goals, decision points
- Decision: does Evidence Bundle v0.1.0 need a new subject type or can skill-variant fit under the existing subject schema? (Documented either way.)
- specs/skill-evolution/v0.1.0-draft/SPEC-DRAFT.md authored — at most a sketch; ships behind 'draft' label
- One concrete worked example: a hand-authored skill diff (before/after SKILL.md) walked through all three eval-platform layers, with the resulting Evidence Bundle JSON committed under tests/fixtures/skill-variant-example/

## Non-goals

- Building a variant generator. Producer-agnostic by design.
- Wiring this into CCP today. Skill format work in CCP (claude-nwdy, claude-22cg, env-var-self-decl bead) is prerequisite for any eval to make sense.
- Production rollout. This is research phase only.

title: M4: lab cross-references for intent-rollout-gate 4th repo
description: Updates intent-eval-lab CLAUDE.md (three-repo → four-repo convergence; tier-1 table; new repo description) + system brief addendum at 000-docs/007-DR-BRIEF-...html (Addendum 2026-05-12 documenting the 4th repo creation, why-not-as-a-module rationale, what-changed-for-§-8, what-changed-for-build-sequence). Also adds projects/intent-rollout-gate symlink (gitignored, local-only). Companion to umbrella CLAUDE.md + README.md edits (those live in non-git intent-eval-platform/ dir).
close_reason: Done. Lab CLAUDE.md updated (three-repo→four-repo + tier-1 row added). System brief addendum landed. Symlink added. PR forthcoming.

title: M4 epic: Provider adapters + Rollout Gate seed (build journey Milestone 4)
description: Per ~/.claude/plans/se-the-council-bubbly-frog.md M4. Sub-deliverables: (1) PB-7 measurement protocol doc [DONE: j-rig PR #52]; (2) intent-rollout-gate 4th repo bootstrap [IN PROGRESS via subagent]; (3) Provider interface in j-rig core; (4) LiteLLM + Vercel AI SDK prototypes per protocol; (5) CISO PASS/FAIL gates implementation; (6) Decision Record locking choice; (7) cross-repo CLAUDE.md updates referencing 4th repo; (8) system brief addendum. Phase 4 of M4 (decision lock) requires actual measurement data from the prototypes. Phases 4-6 are likely subsequent sessions.

close_reason: audit-harness/CLAUDE.md NPM count corrected: 45,000+ → ~1,140 (verified 2026-05-11). 45k figure belonged to claude-code-plugins package per ISEDC Session 3 Research Expert seat factual correction. Correction notes the prior figure inline so future readers see the rationale.

title: M1: OTel SIG-GenAI paper-trail doc + informal email draft
description: Author 000-docs/009-RR-INTL-otel-sig-genai-temperature.md. GC paper-trail file per ISEDC v1 Q4 CSO sequence. Contains: (1) verified maintainer-name identification approach, (2) 2-paragraph email draft (vocabulary overview only, NO RFC enclosed), (3) what counts as response/timeout/triage signal, (4) Week 4+ RFC routing-decision criteria. Stage email for Jeremy to send manually; flag user-action required.
close_reason: 009-RR-INTL-otel-sig-genai-temperature.md authored. Verified maintainer identification approach documented (re-read fresh OTel SIG-GenAI charter + meeting notes; identify maintainer with semantic-conventions PR activity in last 90 days). 2-paragraph email draft staged (~250 words, no RFC enclosure, no Phase B timeline commitments, invites re-routing). Response classification (routing-accept/redirect/defer/no-response/hostile). Week 4+ RFC routing-decision criteria. SEND is a Jeremy manual action.

title: M1: CONTRIBUTING-failure-shape.md per ISEDC v1 Q3 MM-7 deferral path
description: Author specs/mcp-plugin-observability/v0.1.0-draft/CONTRIBUTING-failure-shape.md. Community contribution path for new failure-mode categories (MM-7+). Acceptance: ≥2 independent engagements observe the failure shape, must not type-fit MM-1..MM-6, must include OTel signal vocabulary draft. Per ISEDC v1 Q3 binding constraint.
close_reason: CONTRIBUTING-failure-shape.md authored at specs/mcp-plugin-observability/v0.1.0-draft/. Three admission criteria (C1 ≥2 independent engagements with comparative analysis, C2 type-distinct from MM-1..MM-6, C3 OTel signal vocabulary draft). Submission process: GH issue, 14-day adjudication, written rejection criterion when not admitted. Minor-version landing process documented.

title: M1: FUTURE.md deferred entries — policy peer spec module + MM-7 + OTel collector ref + Pact
description: Update 000-docs/FUTURE.md to add: (1) Policy as peer spec module (trigger: external implementer needs it as standalone spec); (2) MM-7 admission criteria (deferred per ISEDC v1 Q3); (3) OTel collector reference deployment (M6+); (4) Pact extension + Gherkin scenarios (PB-12 wave 2). Each entry: insight, why-not-now, architecture-when-trigger, CISO gates if any, trigger condition. Preserve existing gateway entry.
close_reason: FUTURE.md updated with 4 new deferred entries each carrying trigger conditions: (1) Policy as Peer Spec Module — external implementer OR multi-consumer trigger; (2) MM-7+ Admission Criteria — adjudication of a CONTRIBUTING-failure-shape.md proposal; (3) OTel Collector Reference Deployment — M6 ship + user request; (4) Pact Extension Format + Gherkin Scenarios (PB-12) — PB-11 ship + naming. Preserves existing Gateway entry.

title: M1: 008-DR-GAPS gap-analysis doc
description: Author 000-docs/008-DR-GAPS-spec-vs-system-brief-2026-05-11.md. 10 gap areas: what M1 ships, what stays in FUTURE.md (policy peer spec module, Rollout Gate gateway federation reference, MM-7 admission criteria, OTel collector reference deployment, Pact extension format, Gherkin scenarios, AGENTS.md parser, provider adapter measurement, in-toto subject-naming regex tightening, Rekor anchor verification scripts).
close_reason: 008-DR-GAPS authored. 10 gap areas (G1-G10) enumerated: M1 shipped (G1-G4), DEFERRED to FUTURE.md (G5 policy peer module, G6 gateway, G8 OTel collector ref deploy, G9 Pact/Gherkin), MM-7 CONTRIBUTING shipped + MM-7 itself deferred (G7), M3-M5 deferral (G10).

title: M1: Examples — policy.yaml, evidence-bundle.json, in-toto-statement.json
description: Author informative example files at specs/evidence-bundle/v0.1.0-draft/examples/. policy.yaml is an example tests/TESTING.md shape (NOT normative — consumer-facing config per convergent critique). evidence-bundle.json holds N in-toto Statement v1 rows. in-toto-statement.json is one well-formed signed row.
close_reason: Three example files at examples/: in-toto-statement.json (single PASS row), evidence-bundle.json (6 rows: PASS/ADVISORY/NOT_APPLICABLE/FAIL across audit-harness + j-rig), policy.yaml (informative example tests/TESTING.md shape — explicitly marked NOT normative).

title: M1: JSON Schema gate-result.schema.json
description: Author specs/evidence-bundle/v0.1.0-draft/schema/gate-result.schema.json. Validates the predicate body for predicateType https://evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1. Fields: gate_id, result (PASS/FAIL/ADVISORY/NOT_APPLICABLE), policy_hash, input_hash, timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC), runner, commit_sha, optional metadata. Validates synthetic test rows. Draft 2020-12.
close_reason: JSON Schema gate-result.schema.json authored. Draft 2020-12. Validates required fields (gate_id with regex, result enum, policy_hash + input_hash with sha256 prefix pattern, timestamp date-time, runner with semver pattern, commit_sha 7-40 hex). Optional metadata, failure_mode, advisory_severity. Conditional: ADVISORY result requires advisory_severity.

title: M1: SPEC.md normative — Evidence Bundle envelope, subject naming, signing, policy consumption
description: Extract sections 4-6 of system brief 007-DR-BRIEF into normative SPEC.md at specs/evidence-bundle/v0.1.0-draft/SPEC.md. Replace skeleton with: in-toto Statement v1 wrap, evals.intentsolutions.io/gate-result/v1 predicate URI, Subject Naming section (tool:side:gate-id regex pattern), Signing & Verification section (cosign-compatible, Rekor anchor), Policy consumption section pointing to tests/TESTING.md — NOT a peer policy spec module per convergent critique. RFC 2119 keywords. Cites in-toto, sigstore, OTel, JSON Schema 2020-12.
close_reason: SPEC.md elevated from skeleton to normative draft. 14 sections covering envelope, predicate URI, subject naming regex, signing/verification, policy consumption interface (NOT peer module per critique), version evolution, conformance reporting. RFC 2119 keywords. Cites in-toto, DSSE, cosign, Rekor, JSON Schema 2020-12, OTel.

title: M1 epic: Foundation — Evidence Bundle SPEC normative + schema + examples + DNSSEC + OTel email + paper trail
description: Milestone 1 of the build journey per se-the-council-bubbly-frog.md. Lifts ISEDC Session 3 Q1 gate (paying-customer signal) per Jeremy build-mode directive 2026-05-11. Scope: extract Evidence Bundle SPEC from system brief § 4-6 into normative SPEC.md, ship JSON Schema gate-result.schema.json, ship example artifacts (policy.yaml, evidence-bundle.json, in-toto-statement.json), gap-analysis doc 008-DR-GAPS, FUTURE.md deferred entries (policy peer module, gateway, MM-7, OTel reference deploy), CONTRIBUTING-failure-shape.md per ISEDC v1 Q3, OTel SIG-GenAI informal email + 0NN-RR-INTL paper-trail file per ISEDC v1 Q4, audit-harness CLAUDE.md NPM count correction (45k→~1,140), 000-INDEX.md update. DNSSEC + CAA at Porkbun + actual email send are Jeremy manual actions; this epic stages all artifacts and drafts.
acceptance_criteria: SPEC.md is normative not skeleton; JSON Schema validates synthetic test row; 3 example files exist; 008-DR-GAPS filed; FUTURE.md gains 4 deferred entries with trigger conditions; CONTRIBUTING-failure-shape.md filed in mcp-plugin-observability/v0.1.0-draft/; OTel paper-trail file filed with email draft; audit-harness CLAUDE.md NPM number corrected; 000-INDEX.md indexes new docs; M1 readiness for cosign verify-attestation round-trip after DNSSEC documented as Jeremy action
close_reason: M1 Foundation milestone complete (all 9 artifact sub-beads closed). Shipped: SPEC.md normative draft (14 sections RFC 2119), gate-result.schema.json (Draft 2020-12 validates 7 example predicates), 3 example files (in-toto-statement.json, evidence-bundle.json 6 rows including PASS/FAIL/ADVISORY/NOT_APPLICABLE, informative policy.yaml), 008-DR-GAPS gap analysis (10 areas), FUTURE.md +4 deferred entries with trigger conditions, 009-RR-INTL OTel paper trail with email draft, CONTRIBUTING-failure-shape.md MM-7+ admission gate, 000-INDEX.md updated, audit-harness CLAUDE.md NPM count corrected. Brand-name policy clean. Empirical schema validation: 7/7 example predicates pass. Remaining manual: iel-4zr (DNSSEC + CAA + cosign roundtrip) is the M1->M2 gate; iel-p4a OTel email staged but not sent.
